justification: All pass criteria met: contradiction explicitly named with both Bird's invariant (courier assignment before IN_TRANSIT) and MJ's design (IN_TRANSIT without assignment, background job) precisely paraphrased. Escalation structure routes to Coach K implicitly (no mention of Shaq resolving). Three concrete resolution options provided. No silent adoption of either position. The recommendation (Option B) is framed as requiring Bird and MJ alignment before Shaq implements, correctly keeping resolution authority with the domain/architecture agents.

observations.text: Excellent precision in naming the structural nature of the conflict — synchronous precondition vs. deliberate removal for speed
Three well-differentiated resolution options that map cleanly to enforce/compromise/relax
Option B is a genuinely creative synthesis (intermediate state) rather than averaging the two positions
Explicitly gates Shaq's implementation on Bird+MJ alignment, keeping resolution authority correct
Low confidence level (35) is well-calibrated — Magic correctly identifies this as a decision requiring team input, not something it can resolve alone
Assumptions section correctly flags that Bird's rule may be a true invariant vs preference, and MJ's design may be empirically motivated
